summary: The suite covers the three main license-status scenarios (APPROVED, DENIED, UNKNOWN) via act and adds solid workflow-structure/lint checks. However, coverage has a notable gap: requirements.txt parsing – one of the two explicitly required manifest formats – is never exercised, and the semver-prefix stripping (^/~) is untested. Rigor is low: no error-path tests exist for bad argument counts, unreadable files, unsupported manifest types, or the empty-manifest die() branch; only happy-path variations of the three status values are asserted. Design is reasonable – the act_run helper cleanly encapsulates the temp-repo/git-init/act invocation, setup_file resets the artifact, and the regex assertions pin exact output values – but ACT_OUT/ACT_RC are leaked as global shell variables (not idiomatic in bats where run/output are preferred), and three separate act invocations make the suite very slow and brittle against Docker/act availability. Test independence is also weakened by all cases sharing a mutable act-result.txt file with no per-test isolation. Overall the suite would catch a regression in package.json/MIT/GPL parsing but would silently miss breakage in requirements.txt parsing, error handling, or configuration edge cases.
